name: CFSC Checks Cashed Avenue C
description_short: CFSC operates a multi-location check-cashing network across NYC, New Jersey, and Queens offering no-ID-required check cashing, money transfers, and financial services.
description_long:
Community Financial Services (CFSC) is an established check-cashing and financial services company operating multiple locations throughout the New York metropolitan area, including Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, and New Jersey. The company has built a presence as an alternative to traditional banking for unbanked and underbanked consumers seeking convenient access to financial services without lengthy processing times or account requirements.

CFSC offers a comprehensive suite of services beyond basic check cashing, including money orders, money transfers to over 80 countries, prepaid debit cards (Yes! Debit Account), electronic bill pay, foreign currency exchange, ATM access, and specialty services like tax refund cashing, Transit MetroCard purchases, EZ Pass sales, and lottery services. The Avenue C location at 99 Loisaida Ave operates Monday-Friday 7:30am-8:00pm and Saturday 7:30am-6:00pm, with multilingual support (Se Habla Español) available.

The company distinguishes itself through extended operating hours (some locations open as early as 6:30am and stay open until 9:30pm), no bank account requirement for check cashing, no credit check for prepaid debit card accounts, and acceptance of multiple government-issued photo ID types. CFSC's network model provides customers with 13+ visible locations in the metro area, enabling convenient access regardless of neighborhood. The company explicitly markets to consumers handling paycheck cashing, family money transfers, and bill payments without traditional banking infrastructure.

As a check-cashing service, CFSC fills a legitimate market gap for unbanked populations but operates in a traditionally high-margin industry where fees for services like check cashing and money transfers are standard and typically higher than bank alternatives. The company does not appear to offer credit-building products or financial counseling services. For consumers with banking access, traditional bank services typically cost less; however, for those without accounts or seeking immediate fund access, CFSC provides a practical alternative.
pros: No bank account required for check cashing—immediate access to funds without waiting for bank processing
Extended hours at multiple locations (some open 6:30am-9:30pm), accommodating shift workers and busy schedules
Accepts multiple government-issued photo IDs including passport, foreign country ID, student ID, and work ID
No credit check required for Yes! Debit Account prepaid cards, accessible to those with poor or no credit history
Extensive service menu including foreign currency exchange (80+ countries), money transfers, bill pay, and specialty services
Multilingual support (Se Habla Español) and significant geographic footprint across NYC, Brooklyn, Queens, and New Jersey
Business check cashing available for self-employed and business owners
cons: Check-cashing and money transfer fees not disclosed on website—consumers must inquire in-person, typical industry fees often significantly higher than bank alternatives
Prepaid debit card (Yes! Debit) likely includes monthly maintenance or per-transaction fees common to prepaid products, with no fee details disclosed
No credit-building products or financial counseling services offered—focused on transaction services only, not helping customers improve financial health
Hours vary significantly by location (some 24-hour, others 7:30am-6:00pm)—customers must verify specific branch hours
Limited transparency on exchange rates and conversion fees for foreign currency exchange and international money transfers
best_for: Unbanked workers who receive paychecks and need immediate access to funds without a bank account
Immigrants and international communities needing to send money abroad and exchange foreign currency
Underbanked individuals with poor credit or no credit history seeking prepaid card alternatives
Self-employed and small business owners requiring business check-cashing services outside traditional bank hours
diagnosis: CFSC is best for unbanked and underbanked consumers in the NYC metro area who need fast, no-account-required access to check cashing and money transfer services. The primary caveat is that while CFSC solves the immediate accessibility problem, its service fees (not disclosed online) are typically substantially higher than traditional banks, making it a convenience premium rather than a cost-effective long-term financial solution.
